CREATE TABLE test_long_varchar (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data LONG VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_long_varchar (id, data) VALUES (1, 'Sample data for testing LONG VARCHAR type.');
INSERT INTO test_long_varchar (id, data) VALUES (2, REPEAT('A', 65535));
INSERT INTO test_long_varchar (id, data) VALUES (3, REPEAT('B', 70000));
SELECT * FROM test_long_varchar WHERE data LIKE '%Sample%';
SELECT id FROM test_long_varchar WHERE LENGTH(data) > 50000;
CREATE TABLE test_other_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, description LONG VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_other_table (id, description) VALUES (1, 'Another table with LONG VARCHAR');
SELECT a.id, a.data FROM test_long_varchar a JOIN test_other_table b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT a.id FROM test_long_varchar a WHERE a.data IN (SELECT description FROM test_other_table WHERE LENGTH(description) < 200);
CREATE TABLE test_complex_join (id INT PRIMARY KEY, long_data LONG VARCHAR, other_id INT);
INSERT INTO test_complex_join (id, long_data, other_id) VALUES (1, 'Complex test entry', 1);
SELECT a.id, a.long_data FROM test_complex_join a LEFT JOIN test_long_varchar b ON a.other_id = b.id WHERE LENGTH(a.long_data) > 10;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_long_varchar WHERE data IS NOT NULL AND id BETWEEN 1 AND 3;
INSERT INTO test_long_varchar (id, data) VALUES (4, 'Edge case for LONG VARCHAR with very long input: ' || REPEAT('C', 65535));
SELECT id FROM test_long_varchar WHERE data IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_other_table WHERE id = test_long_varchar.id);
CREATE TABLE test_long_varchar_nested (id INT PRIMARY KEY, nested_data LONG VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_long_varchar_nested (id, nested_data) VALUES (1, (SELECT data FROM test_long_varchar WHERE id = 1));
